name: MinimizOR
statement:
You are given an array $a$ of $n$ non-negative integers, numbered from $1$ to $n$.
Let's define the cost of the array $a$ as $\displaystyle \min_{i \neq j} a_i | a_j$, where $|$ denotes the bitwise OR operation.
There are $q$ queries. For each query you are given two integers $l$ and $r$ ($l  \lt  r$). For each query you should find the cost of the subarray $a_{l}, a_{l + 1}, \ldots, a_{r}$.
input:
Each test case consists of several test cases. The first line contains a single integer $t$ ($1 \le t \le 10^4$)¬†‚ÄĒ the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer $n$ ($2 \le n \le 10^5$)¬†‚ÄĒ the length array $a$.
The second line of each test case contains $n$ integers $a_1, a_2, \ldots, a_n$ ($0 \le a_i  \lt  2^{30}$)¬†‚ÄĒ the elements of $a$.
The third line of each test case contains an integer $q$ ($1 \le q \le 10^5$)¬†‚ÄĒ the number of queries.
Each of the next $q$ lines contains two integers $l_j$, $r_j$ ($1 \le l_j  \lt  r_j \le n$)¬†‚ÄĒ the description of the $j$-th query.
It is guaranteed that the sum of $n$ and the sum of $q$ over all test cases do not exceed $10^5$.
output: For each test case print $q$ numbers, where the $j$-th number is the cost of array $a_{l_j}, a_{l_j + 1}, \ldots, a_{r_j}$.
